label: correctness_bug_off_by_one
summary: The response shape, default page size, query param parsing, and 400 validation all meet their acceptance criteria. However, the skip formula `skip: page * pageSize` has a critical off-by-one bug: page=1 computes skip=20, skipping the first 20 items entirely and returning the second page instead of the first. Criterion 3 (page=1 returns first page) is directly violated. The fix is mechanical — change one operator — but it must be accompanied by a test that asserts page=1 produces the first record, not item at offset 20. Test changes listed in filesChanged were absent from the diff, so test coverage of this exact path cannot be verified.

findings.description: Off-by-one in skip formula: `skip: page * pageSize` produces skip=20 when page=1, skipping the entire first page and making page=1 return items 21-40 instead of 1-20.
findings.suggestion: Change to `skip: (page - 1) * pageSize` so that page=1 produces skip=0, returning the first pageSize records.

findings.description: Completion signal lists tests/routes/items.test.ts as changed but no test diff was provided; it is unverifiable whether the off-by-one skip or the page=1 contract is covered by tests.
findings.suggestion: Include test file changes in the diff and add an assertion that page=1 returns the first record (index 0), not the second page.

fullReview.summary: The response shape, default page size, query param parsing, and 400 validation all meet their acceptance criteria. However, the skip formula `skip: page * pageSize` has a critical off-by-one bug: page=1 computes skip=20, skipping the first 20 items entirely and returning the second page instead of the first. Criterion 3 (page=1 returns first page) is directly violated. The fix is mechanical — change one operator — but it must be accompanied by a test that asserts page=1 produces the first record, not item at offset 20. Test changes listed in filesChanged were absent from the diff, so test coverage of this exact path cannot be verified.

criteriaAssessment.evidence: Both params are read from req.query and parsed with parseInt; absent params fall back to string defaults '1' and '20' respectively.
`req.query.pageSize ?? '20'` correctly defaults pageSize to 20 when the query param is absent.

criteriaAssessment.criterion: Page numbers are 1-indexed (page=1 returns first page)
criteriaAssessment.evidence: `skip: page * pageSize` computes skip=20 for page=1, returning items at offset 20-39 instead of 0-19. The first page is unreachable: page=0 is the only value that would produce skip=0, but page=0 is rejected by the `page < 1` guard.

criteriaAssessment.criterion: Response includes { items, totalCount, page, pageSize, totalPages }
criteriaAssessment.evidence: All five fields are present in the res.json() call; totalPages is computed via Math.ceil(totalCount / pageSize).
Validation block checks isNaN, page < 1, pageSize < 1, and pageSize > 100; any failing condition returns status(400) with an error JSON body.
